summary: Ukrainian drones struck infrastructure in occupied Crimea and Russia's Krasnodar region, causing fires and damaging the Chongar railway bridge, while Russian forces used FAB‑3000 bombs to hit Ukrainian dislocation points in the DPR and Ukrainian forces hit an oil‑processing facility in Volgograd. Multiple KAB attacks were reported across Dnipropetrovsk, Kherson and Mykolaiv regions, causing injuries and material damage. Drone activity continues across northern, eastern and southern fronts.
key_points: Ukrainian drones attacked Crimea and Krasnodar, igniting fires at Temryuk sea terminal and nearby warehouses, and damaged the Chongar railway bridge, halting traffic (Source: @eRadarrua/96377).
Russian pilots bombed temporary Ukrainian dislocation points in the Donetsk People's Republic with FAB‑3000 bombs (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30875).
Ukrainian forces struck the oil‑preparation and pumping plant near Kotovo in Volgograd region, confirming a fire at the facility (Source: @UkraineNow/81894).
Twelve KABs hit Vasylkivka in Dnipropetrovsk, injuring nine people, damaging a market, eight shops and a multi‑storey building (Source: @UkraineNow/81890).
KAB attacks were reported on the western side of Kherson and the eastern side of Mykolaiv regions (Source: @kpszsu/64963).
Repeated KAB launches were noted on the eastern side of Dnipropetrovsk (Source: @kpszsu/65005).

Numerous UAV flights were observed from Chernihiv toward northern Kyiv, from the north of Kharkiv toward the south, and from the south toward Zaporizhzhia (Source: @kpszsu/65010, @kpszsu/65003, @kpszsu/65014).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian drones struck key infrastructure in occupied Crimea and Russia's Krasnodar region, causing fires and closing the Chongar railway bridge. Russian forces employed FAB‑3000 bombs against Ukrainian dislocation points in the DPR, while Ukrainian forces hit an oil‑processing plant in Volgograd. Multiple KAB attacks across Dnipropetrovsk, Kherson and Mykolaiv resulted in injuries and significant material damage.

points: Ukrainian drones targeted the Temryuk sea terminal in Krasnodar, igniting a fire, and also struck warehouses and truck parking areas, prompting emergency services to respond (Source: @eRadarrua/96377).

A Ukrainian strike hit the oil‑preparation and pumping facility near Kotovo in Volgograd region, confirming a fire at the plant that processes oil from Volgograd, Astrakhan and Kalmykia (Source: @UkraineNow/81894).

Ukrainian forces launched twelve KABs on Vasylkivka, Dnipropetrovsk, injuring nine civilians, damaging a local market, eight shops, ten trade pavilions and a multi‑storey residential building (Source: @UkraineNow/81890).
Repeated KAB launches were reported on the eastern side of Dnipropetrovsk, indicating sustained strike capability in that sector (Source: @kpszsu/65005).
Ukrainian UAV activity was observed from Chernihiv heading north toward Kyiv, suggesting reconnaissance or strike missions near the capital (Source: @kpszsu/65010).
UAVs were also reported flying from the north of Kharkiv toward the south, indicating pressure on the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/65003).
UAVs from the south moved toward Zaporizhzhia, showing operational reach into the southern industrial zone (Source: @kpszsu/65014).
Russian pilots used FAB‑3000 aerial bombs to destroy temporary Ukrainian dislocation points in the Donetsk People's Republic, degrading Ukrainian command and control (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30875).
Russian authorities reported that drones of the "Oblomok" type caused a fire at the Temryuk sea terminal in Krasnodar (Source: @eRadarrua/96377).
The governor of Krasnodar region confirmed one fatality and three injuries from a UAV attack in the region (Source: @tass_agency/380462).
Russian forces launched approximately eight KABs over Kherson in the morning, with outcomes not yet reported (Source: @UkraineNow/81890).
Threats of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from occupied Crimea were announced, though no launch was confirmed (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67581).
Russian officials claimed that Ukrainian strikes cannot help the enemy, a political statement without operational detail (Source: @tass_agency/380488).
Russian media reported fuel limits at certain stations in occupied territories, indicating logistical constraints (Source: @eRadarrua/96389).
Ukrainian UAVs from the north of Kharkiv moved southward, indicating pressure on Russian positions in the eastern sector (Source: @kpszsu/65003).
Repeated KAB launches on the eastern side of Dnipropetrovsk suggest sustained Ukrainian strike activity against Russian logistics (Source: @kpszsu/65005).
UAVs observed from the Chernihiv area heading toward Kyiv may be part of broader eastern‑to‑central operations (Source: @kpszsu/65010).
Russian FAB‑3000 bomb strikes on Ukrainian dislocation points in the DPR represent a direct counter‑strike on eastern Ukrainian forces (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30875).
KAB attacks on the western side of Kherson and eastern Mykolaiv could affect supply lines feeding the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/64963).
UAV activity reported from the north of Chernihiv along the Belarus border moving toward Kyiv indicates potential threat to rear areas of the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/64964).
UAVs from the south of Mykolaiv moving north could target eastern supply routes (Source: @kpszsu/65011).
UAVs were observed flying from Chernihiv toward the north of Kyiv, suggesting reconnaissance or strike missions near the capital (Source: @kpszsu/65010).
A group of UAVs moved along the Belarus border from northern Chernihiv toward Kyiv, indicating pressure on northern approaches (Source: @kpszsu/64964).
UAVs reported from the north of Chernihiv heading to Dobryanka show activity along the northern front (Source: @eRadarrua/96349).
UAVs from the north of Chernihiv were also seen heading toward Slavutych, indicating broader northern coverage (Source: @eRadarrua/96368).
No confirmed aerial engagements were reported, but multiple UAV sightings suggest heightened surveillance activity (multiple sources).
Air raid alerts in Kyiv at 22:30 and 12:12 indicate ongoing air defense activity over the northern capital (Source: @eRadarrua/96344, @eRadarrua/96341).
No casualties were reported from these northern UAV movements.
UAVs from the south of Mykolaiv moved northward, potentially targeting logistics in the southern theater (Source: @kpszsu/65011).
UAVs from the south heading toward Zaporizhzhia indicate pressure on the southern industrial corridor (Source: @kpszsu/65014).
KAB attacks on western Kherson and eastern Mykolaiv regions suggest intensified strikes on southern supply lines (Source: @kpszsu/64963).
Russian KAB launches over Kherson (about eight) were reported in the morning, though damage details are pending (Source: @UkraineNow/81890).
UAVs observed over the Black Sea heading toward Chornomorsk and further to Tatarbunari indicate maritime‑linked operations in the south (Source: @eRadarrua/96356).
A UAV incident in Mykolaiv caused injuries to two civilians from falling debris (Source: @UkraineNow/81890).
Fuel restrictions at occupied stations in the south reflect logistical strain (Source: @eRadarrua/96389).
Ukrainian drones intercepted 177 units across Crimea and Krasnodar, with many causing fires and infrastructure damage (Source: @eRadarrua/96377).
Russian air defenses claimed to have downed 110 of 118 Ukrainian attack drones during a recent night (Source: @UkraineNow/81890).
Multiple KAB attacks were reported across Dnipropetrovsk, Kherson and Mykolaiv, causing civilian injuries and property damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81890, @kpszsu/64963).
UAV sightings across northern, eastern and southern fronts indicate widespread aerial activity, though specific engagements are limited (multiple sources).
Air raid alerts in Kyiv at 22:30 and 12:12 reflect ongoing threat perception and active air defense alerts (Source: @eRadarrua/96344, @eRadarrua/96341).
Russian pilots employed FAB‑3000 bombs, a heavy aerial munition, against Ukrainian positions in the DPR (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30875).
Threats of Iskander‑M missile launches from occupied Crimea were announced, indicating potential escalation (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67581).

key_developments: Ukrainian drones caused fires at Temryuk sea terminal and damaged the Chongar railway bridge, halting traffic between Crimea and occupied Kherson (Source: @eRadarrua/96377).
Russian pilots used FAB‑3000 bombs to destroy Ukrainian temporary dislocation points in the Donetsk People's Republic (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30875).
Ukrainian strike ignited a fire at the oil‑preparation plant near Kotovo in Volgograd region (Source: @UkraineNow/81894).
Twelve KABs hit Vasylkivka, Dnipropetrovsk, injuring nine civilians and damaging market infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/81890).
KAB attacks on western Kherson and eastern Mykolaiv indicate expanding Ukrainian strike reach in the south (Source: @kpszsu/64963).
UAV activity reported from Chernihiv toward Kyiv and from the north of Kharkiv toward the south shows multi‑directional aerial pressure (Source: @kpszsu/65010, @kpszsu/65003).
Threat of Iskander‑M missile launches from occupied Crimea was announced, raising escalation risk (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67581).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian aerial operations are intensifying, with drones targeting critical logistics in occupied Crimea, Krasnodar and southern Russia, while KAB strikes are causing civilian casualties and infrastructure damage across multiple fronts. Russian use of heavy FAB‑3000 bombs in the DPR demonstrates a capability to strike deep Ukrainian positions. The announced possibility of Iskander‑M missile launches from occupied Crimea adds a strategic ballistic threat that could alter the operational calculus on the southern front.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities are reinforcing air defenses, as evidenced by high interception rates of Ukrainian drones and increased PVO activity around Kyiv. Russian regional governors are reporting damage and casualties, prompting emergency responses and potential reinforcement of air defense assets in Krasnodar and Kherson. Both sides appear to be reallocating assets to protect critical infrastructure and supply routes amid the heightened aerial contest.
sections.intelligence_notes: Continued monitoring of UAV flight paths is essential to anticipate strike vectors, especially from northern Chernihiv toward Kyiv and from the south toward Zaporizhzhia. The use of FAB‑3000 bombs suggests Russian air assets are being employed for precision strikes on Ukrainian command nodes. Open-source reports of fuel limits in occupied territories may indicate logistical strain that could affect Russian operational tempo.
